bordeaux.name: Médoc Grand Cru Left-Bank Route
bordeaux.summary: A classic Cabernet Sauvignon pilgrimage up the D2 'Route des Châteaux', from the elegant gravel terroirs of Margaux to the powerhouse First and Second Growths of Pauillac and Saint-Estèphe.

stops.name: Château Prieuré-Lichine
stops.visit: Mon-Sat, guided tours & tastings
stops.note: Welcoming 4th Growth, e-bike vineyard tours

stops.name: Château Marquis de Terme
stops.visit: Tours & tastings by booking
stops.note: Intimate 4th Growth with food-pairing experiences

stops.name: Château Lynch-Bages
stops.visit: Mon-Sat, shop, cellars & tastings
stops.note: Visitor-friendly 5th Growth beside Bages village

stops.name: Château Pichon Baron
stops.visit: Private tours year-round by reservation
stops.note: Fairytale moated Second Growth château

stops.name: Château Mouton Rothschild
stops.visit: By appointment (book well ahead), Mon-Fri
stops.note: First Growth with the Museum of Wine in Art

stops.name: Château Cos d'Estournel
stops.visit: Tours & tastings by appointment
stops.note: Exotic pagoda-topped Second Growth on the Pauillac border

bordeaux.name: Saint-Émilion & Pomerol Right-Bank Route
bordeaux.summary: A Merlot-led tour of the UNESCO-listed limestone plateau and its hidden Pomerol neighbour, mixing village cellars, hilltop estates and a Jean Nouvel rooftop terrace.

stops.name: Château Guadet
stops.visit: Family-run tours & tastings by booking
stops.note: Organic estate with cellars beneath the medieval village

stops.name: Château Franc Mayne
stops.visit: Daily tours, underground quarry galleries
stops.note: Grand Cru Classé with dramatic limestone caves

stops.name: Château Troplong Mondot
stops.visit: Private tours by appointment
stops.note: Premier Grand Cru Classé atop the plateau's highest point

stops.name: Château La Dominique
stops.visit: Tours, tastings & rooftop restaurant
stops.note: Jean Nouvel red-glass cellar overlooking Pomerol

stops.name: Château Beauregard
stops.visit: Private tours & tastings by appointment
stops.note: Rare Pomerol estate that genuinely welcomes visitors

bordeaux.name: Graves & Sauternes Southern Route
bordeaux.summary: Bordeaux's historic southern heartland: red-and-white Grand Crus of Pessac-Léognan one day, then the golden botrytised sweet wines of Sauternes the next.

stops.name: Château Pape Clément
stops.visit: Tours & tastings daily by booking
stops.note: Seven centuries of history in Bordeaux's suburbs

stops.name: Château Carbonnieux
stops.visit: Tours & tastings by appointment
stops.note: Monastic estate renowned for crisp white Graves

stops.name: Château Smith Haut Lafitte
stops.visit: Tours, art forest, spa & restaurant
stops.note: Organic Grand Cru Classé with the Caudalie vinothérapie spa

stops.name: Château Latour-Martillac
stops.visit: Tours & tastings by booking
stops.note: Family-run Grand Cru Classé for reds and whites

stops.name: Château Guiraud
stops.visit: Open daily 10-18h, with or without booking
stops.note: Organic Premier Cru Classé with Michelin-starred restaurant

bourgogne.name: Côte de Beaune Grand Classics
bourgogne.summary: The historic négociant cellars of Beaune paired with two château estates to the south, covering both legendary Pinot Noir and white Meursault terroirs.

stops.name: Maison Patriarche Père & Fils
stops.visit: Daily self-guided cellar tour with tasting, no booking needed
stops.note: 5 km of candle-lit cellars, the largest in Burgundy, tasting a flight of Côte d'Or appellations

stops.name: Bouchard Père & Fils
stops.visit: By appointment
stops.note: Cellars beneath the 15th-c. Château de Beaune; taste Beaune Premier Cru from one of Burgundy's largest domaines

stops.name: Maison Joseph Drouhin
stops.visit: Guided tours at set times, booking advised
stops.note: Ancient cellars under old Beaune; tasting of emblematic cuvées including Chablis and Côte d'Or

stops.name: Château de Pommard
stops.visit: Guided tours and tastings, booking required
stops.note: Monopole Clos Marey-Monge; structured Pommard reds with trained guides

stops.name: Château de Meursault
stops.visit: Open daily; tours and salon tastings, reservation advised
stops.note: Grand 14th-c. cellars and a benchmark flight of white Meursault and Côte de Beaune crus

bourgogne.name: Côte de Nuits Pinot Noir Trail
bourgogne.summary: A south-to-north drive through the great red-wine communes of the Côte de Nuits, from Nuits-Saint-Georges up to Marsannay at the gates of Dijon.

stops.name: L'Imaginarium
stops.visit: Open daily; interactive museum plus tasting, booking advised
stops.note: Boisset's sparkling and still-wine discovery centre, ideal intro to Burgundy method and terroir

stops.name: Domaine Manuel Olivier
stops.visit: Tasting cellar, by appointment
stops.note: Family domaine pouring Côte de Nuits, Côte de Beaune and Hautes-Côtes side by side

stops.name: Château du Clos de Vougeot
stops.visit: Open daily for guided/self-guided visits
stops.note: Cistercian Grand Cru monument and historic wine presses, heart of the Côte de Nuits

stops.name: Château de Gevrey-Chambertin
stops.visit: Guided tours with tasting, by appointment
stops.note: Medieval cellar in the village of the Chambertin Grands Crus; powerful village and 1er Cru reds

stops.name: Château de Marsannay
stops.visit: Open daily; tastings and tours, booking advised
stops.note: Northern gateway estate known for rosé and approachable Marsannay reds

beaujolais.name: Crus du Beaujolais Loop
beaujolais.summary: A two-day sweep of the ten Beaujolais crus, from the granite hills of Brouilly in the south up to Fleurie and Moulin-à-Vent in the north.

stops.name: Château de la Chaize
stops.visit: Open for tours and tastings, booking advised
stops.note: Grand 17th-c. estate with a vast vaulted cellar; flagship Brouilly from a single large vineyard

stops.name: Château Thivin
stops.visit: By appointment
stops.note: Classic family domaine on the slopes of Mont Brouilly; reference Côte de Brouilly

stops.name: Château des Ravatys
stops.visit: By appointment
stops.note: Historic 19th-c. estate at the foot of Mont Brouilly, Brouilly and Côte de Brouilly

stops.name: Cave de Fleurie
stops.visit: Shop and tasting open most days, no booking needed
stops.note: Cooperative pouring the floral, silky 'queen of Beaujolais' cru Fleurie

stops.name: Le Hameau Duboeuf
stops.visit: Open daily; ticketed wine park with tasting
stops.note: France's largest wine theme park, ending with a tasting flight across the Beaujolais crus

stops.name: Château du Moulin-à-Vent
stops.visit: Tours and tastings, by appointment
stops.note: Iconic windmill estate; structured, age-worthy Moulin-à-Vent, the most powerful cru

champagne.name: Grandes Marques: Reims & Épernay Cellars
champagne.summary: A two-day tour of Champagne's most famous houses, descending into UNESCO-listed chalk crayères in Reims before tracing the grand mansions of Épernay's Avenue de Champagne. Pre-book all visits online.

stops.name: Taittinger
stops.visit: Guided tour of Gallo-Roman chalk crayères + tasting
stops.note: Cellars built into 4th-century chalk pits beneath Saint-Nicaise

stops.name: Veuve Clicquot
stops.visit: Guided crayères tour with cuvée tasting (seasonal)
stops.note: 24 km of UNESCO chalk cellars; book well ahead

stops.name: Pommery
stops.visit: Tour of monumental cellars + contemporary art
stops.note: Carved 18 m down with a dramatic sculpted staircase

stops.name: G.H. Mumm
stops.visit: Cellar tour and Cordon Rouge tasting
stops.note: Central Reims, walkable from the cathedral

stops.name: Moët & Chandon
stops.visit: Tour of 28 km of cellars + tasting
stops.note: The flagship house on the Avenue de Champagne

stops.name: Mercier
stops.visit: Laser-guided underground train through the cellars
stops.note: Family-friendly; home of the giant 200,000-bottle foudre

stops.name: Pol Roger
stops.visit: Intimate visit and tasting, by appointment
stops.note: Churchill's favourite; small-group, advance booking essential

champagne.name: Côte des Blancs Blanc de Blancs Growers
champagne.summary: A relaxed grower stretch south of Épernay through the pure-Chardonnay chalk villages, with personal cellar visits at family domaines a fraction of the grandes marques' scale.

stops.name: Champagne Pierre Moncuit
stops.visit: Family-run Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs tasting, by appointment
stops.note: Same Grand Cru village as Salon and Clos du Mesnil

stops.name: Champagne Launois Père & Fils
stops.visit: Cellar visit with Champagne press museum + tasting
stops.note: Remarkable collection of antique presses and tools

stops.name: Champagne Veuve Fourny & Fils
stops.visit: Vineyard, cellar and Premier Cru tasting, by appointment
stops.note: Active family domaine since 1856 at the southern tip of the côte

alsace.name: Route des Vins: Colmar to Riquewihr
alsace.summary: Two days winding through the prettiest villages of the Alsace Wine Route, mixing welcoming walk-in tasting rooms with appointment-only biodynamic domaines among half-timbered houses and Grand Cru hillsides.

stops.name: Domaine Emile Beyer
stops.visit: Tasting room in a 16th-century house on the central square
stops.note: Family estate since 1580 in one of France's loveliest villages

stops.name: Maison Léon Beyer
stops.visit: Tasting room for the famously dry Rieslings and Gewurztraminers
stops.note: Historic Eguisheim négociant-vigneron

stops.name: Maison Hugel (Hugel & Fils)
stops.visit: Walk-in tasting, no booking needed
stops.note: Established 1639; centre of the walled village

stops.name: Domaine Bott-Geyl
stops.visit: Free tasting for private visitors; tours by reservation
stops.note: Organic/biodynamic estate with six Grands Crus

stops.name: Maison Trimbach
stops.visit: Tasting room for the legendary Clos Ste-Hune Riesling
stops.note: Benchmark Alsace house, trading since 1626

loire.name: Touraine Troglodyte Route: Vouvray, Chinon & Bourgueil
loire.summary: A Chenin-and-Cabernet-Franc loop through the tufa hills west of Tours, tasting in 10th-century troglodyte cellars carved into the limestone. Sparkling and dry Vouvray, then the reds of Chinon and Bourgueil.

stops.name: Maison Brédif (Marc Brédif)
stops.visit: Guided tour of 10th-century troglodyte cellars ending with a tasting
stops.note: Book ahead; tour + tasting

stops.name: Château Gaudrelle
stops.visit: Cellar visit and tasting across the Vouvray range, dry to moelleux and sparkling
stops.note: Friendly small-grower stop on the Vouvray plateau

stops.name: Domaine Couly-Dutheil
stops.visit: Walk-in tasting cellar at Clos de l'Écho beside the fortress
stops.note: Historic estate at the foot of Chinon castle

stops.name: Domaine de la Chevalerie
stops.visit: Appointment visit of one of Touraine's largest cellars, free vertical tasting
stops.note: Biodynamic Bourgueil since 1640, by appointment

loire.name: Centre-Loire Sancerre & Chavignol Route
loire.summary: A compact hilltop circuit around Sancerre and the goat-cheese village of Chavignol, pairing crisp Sauvignon Blanc and Pinot Noir with panoramic vineyard views.

stops.name: Maison des Sancerre
stops.visit: Interpretive centre with 4D and 360° vineyard experiences, guided tasting
stops.note: Great orientation stop; open Apr-Nov

stops.name: Domaine Vacheron
stops.visit: Cellar visit and tasting of biodynamic Sancerre Blanc and Rouge
stops.note: Benchmark organic/biodynamic grower in town

stops.name: Domaine Henri Bourgeois
stops.visit: Large visitor centre in Chavignol with complimentary walk-in tastings
stops.note: Open 7 days year-round; cellar tours Apr-Oct

languedoc.name: Pic Saint-Loup & Terrasses du Larzac Garrigue Route
languedoc.summary: Inland from Montpellier into the wild garrigue beneath the Pic Saint-Loup, tasting structured Syrah-Grenache reds at historic estates framed by Mediterranean scrubland.

stops.name: Château de Lascaux
stops.visit: Estate and 12th-century priory cellar visit, tasting of five Pic Saint-Loup wines
stops.note: Organic and biodynamic, garrigue setting

stops.name: Mas de Daumas Gassac
stops.visit: Tour of the wild valley and underground cellars, tasting of the Grands Vins
stops.note: The cult 'Lafite of the Languedoc'; visits in EN/FR

languedoc.name: Côte Vermeille: Banyuls, Collioure & La Clape
languedoc.summary: The seaside fortified-wine coast where terraced vines tumble to the Mediterranean. Taste sweet Banyuls and Collioure reds in cliffside cellars, then a La Clape estate on the road back toward Narbonne.

stops.name: Terres des Templiers (Cellier des Templiers)
stops.visit: Free film, guided tour of the ageing cellars, commented tasting of the three AOPs
stops.note: Flagship Banyuls co-operative

stops.name: Cave l'Étoile
stops.visit: Tasting and cellar visit at the historic 1921 growers' co-operative
stops.note: Old-school oxidative Banyuls in outdoor solera jars

stops.name: Domaine du Mas Blanc (Dr Parcé)
stops.visit: Tasting at the sea-view cellar with single-vineyard Collioure and traditional Banyuls
stops.note: Patriarch estate behind the Collioure appellation

stops.name: Château l'Hospitalet (Gérard Bertrand)
stops.visit: Tasting boutique open daily showcasing La Clape and Languedoc cuvées
stops.note: Wine-resort estate in the La Clape massif

rhone.name: Northern Rhône Syrah & Viognier
rhone.summary: Granite-hill Syrah from Hermitage plus Côte-Rôtie and Condrieu Viognier, walking the famous slopes between tastings. Big-name houses with proper tasting rooms, two estates by appointment.

stops.name: Cave de Tain
stops.visit: Free walk-in boutique tasting facing the Hermitage hill
stops.note: Top co-op, great value

stops.name: Maison M. Chapoutier
stops.visit: Tasting room and bookable vineyard tours with sommelier
stops.note: Biodynamic flagship

stops.name: Vineum Paul Jaboulet Aîné
stops.visit: Wine bar and cellar tasting, La Chapelle verticals
stops.note: Closed Mon-Tue

stops.name: E. Guigal – Caveau du Château
stops.visit: Cellar tour and tasting at Château d'Ampuis
stops.note: Book ahead

stops.name: Domaine Georges Vernay
stops.visit: Benchmark Condrieu Viognier tasting
stops.note: By appointment only

rhone.name: Châteauneuf-du-Pape & Gigondas Grenache
rhone.summary: Galets-pebble Grenache blends in the papal village, then the Dentelles de Montmirail for Gigondas. Mix of walk-in village caveaux and prestige estates by appointment.

stops.name: Château Mont-Redon
stops.visit: Caveau tasting, optional cellar visit with plateau views
stops.note: No appointment for tasting

stops.name: La Cave Famille Perrin (Beaucastel)
stops.visit: Village shop tasting of 5 wines daily
stops.note: Walk-in, Place du Portail

stops.name: Domaine du Vieux Télégraphe
stops.visit: La Crau estate tasting on the high plateau
stops.note: By appointment

stops.name: Château de Beaucastel
stops.visit: Biodynamic estate tour, all 13 varieties
stops.note: By appointment, book 2-3 weeks ahead

stops.name: Caveau du Gigondas
stops.visit: Appellation tasting room, 100+ Gigondas at cellar prices
stops.note: Walk-in, year-round

provence.name: Bandol Mourvèdre & Cassis Whites
provence.summary: Age-worthy Mourvèdre reds and serious rosé on Bandol's sea-facing terraces, then crisp coastal whites in the cliffside amphitheatre of Cassis. Two Bandol icons plus a Calanques estate.

stops.name: Domaine Tempier
stops.visit: Tour and tasting of legendary Bandol reds with charcuterie
stops.note: Call for appointment

stops.name: Château de Pibarnon
stops.visit: Hilltop tasting room with panoramic vineyard views
stops.note: Reservation required, free for up to 8

stops.name: Clos Sainte Magdeleine
stops.visit: Seaside-terroir Cassis white tasting and sales
stops.note: Small estate by the Calanques

provence.name: Côtes de Provence Rosé Trail
provence.summary: The world's most famous Provence rosés, from Gassin near Saint-Tropez to the inland Var estates. Polished tasting rooms welcoming walk-ins and booked visits.

stops.name: Château Minuty
stops.visit: Modern tasting room, full white/rosé/red range
stops.note: Walk-in, L'essentiel tasting

stops.name: Château d'Esclans
stops.visit: Discovery or Prestige visit, home of Whispering Angel and Garrus
stops.note: Book the guided visit

stops.name: Domaines Ott – Château de Selle
stops.visit: Tasting in contemporary cellar of the iconic skittle bottle
stops.note: Dedicated tasting space
